guidance: Check that architecture docs still match the real runtime behavior and rollout assumptions.
Check turn ownership, async continuation, final delivery, and duplicate-send behavior.
Look for restart/recovery regressions, state cleanup races, and changes in reply semantics.
Check mention/topic routing, formatting, media grouping, replay, and message-loss risk.
Check channel-specific formatting, webhook semantics, and compatibility with existing Slack behavior.
Check routing, suppression rules, parse/format behavior, and per-channel delivery semantics.
Pay attention to replay, idempotency, and message-loss risk.
Check startup wiring, ingress/recovery ordering, and durable spool/replay behavior.
Check inbound/outbound durability, ack/release semantics, and replay ordering.
Check scheduling semantics, duplicate execution risk, and silent/visible job behavior.
Check secret storage, redaction, and compatibility with existing credential loading.
Check tool result contracts, async delivery, task registry integration, and user-visible tool semantics.
Check transport lifecycle, tool exposure rules, and failure handling.
Check compaction correctness, summary persistence, and context reconstruction behavior.
Check session identity, reset semantics, persistence boundaries, and cross-channel aliasing behavior.
Check persistent state layout, migration risk, and state ownership invariants.
Check workspace isolation, path resolution, and cross-profile leakage risk.
Check durable task state transitions, terminal state semantics, and projection/reporting contracts.
Check token handling, refresh flows, secret exposure, and persistence of credentials.
Check routing precedence, session targeting, and compatibility with existing dispatch rules.
Check lifecycle/state-machine behavior and whether draft/live transitions remain coherent.
Check one-way migration safety, idempotency, and upgrade compatibility for existing installs.
Check externally reachable API changes, validation, auth boundaries, and migration compatibility.
Check user-visible regressions, API contract drift with backend, and broken workflow affordances.
Check token lifecycle, refresh behavior, callback handling, and secret persistence.
Check provider protocol compatibility, request/response mapping, and tool/media contract behavior.
Check OpenAI Responses API parsing, truncation, and compatibility across provider paths.
Check provider fallback behavior, request/response parsing, and model/tool contract changes.
Check env/config compatibility, default values, and upgrade risk for existing deployments.
Check command-surface compatibility, behavior changes, and user-facing semantics.
Check CLI compatibility and operational behavior changes.
Check CI behavior changes, branch protection expectations, and whether local reviewer guidance needs updating.
Verify tests actually cover the changed behavior and would fail before the change.
